item.title: 14,000 litres of fuel arrive in Taveuni amid ongoing shortage
item.description: Suncity Supermarket owner Rohil Chaudhari confirmed the arrival of the shipment to fijivillage News. The shipment includes unleaded petrol, diesel, premix and kerosene.
item.content:encoded:
Suncity Supermarket owner Rohil Chaudhari confirmed the arrival of the shipment to fijivillage News.

The shipment includes unleaded petrol, diesel, premix and kerosene.

It comes after Chaudhari worked with Pacific Energy to secure the fuel as shipping disruptions left Taveuni facing shortages.

Chaudhari had earlier said the fuel would be distributed manually using measured containers because his service station pumps remain out of operation following a fire.

He says customers could initially be limited to about 20 litres each so more people can access fuel.

item.title: From shaping Fiji's school curriculum to aspiring political leader: Pulotu Rika to appear on fijivillage Her-Story
item.description: From helping shape Fiji's school curriculum to empowering women in communities, former educator Pulotu Rika has dedicated decades of her life to education and service. Now 68, the mother, grandmother and recent widow is embracing a new chapter as one of the women undergoing training to contest the upcoming General Elections, driven by a desire to continue serving Fiji through leadership.
item.content:encoded:
From helping shape Fiji's school curriculum to empowering women in communities, former educator Pulotu Rika has dedicated decades of her life to education and service.

Now 68, the mother, grandmother and recent widow is embracing a new chapter as one of the women undergoing training to contest the upcoming General Elections, driven by a desire to continue serving Fiji through leadership.

You can watch fijivillage Her-Story With Temalesi on our facebook page, YouTube channel, and TikTok and X account, fijivillage at 7pm tomorrow.

You can also catch the podcast, fijivillage Her-Story With Temalesi Vono on Spotify and Apple Podcasts.

item.title: FHTA calls for urgent Govt intervention to address growing fuel and shipping crisis affecting Taveuni
item.description: The Fiji Hotel and Tourism Association is calling for urgent Government intervention to address the growing fuel and shipping cris

item.title: China test-launches a ballistic missile in the South Pacific and raises regional concerns, Australia says this is destabilizing to the region
item.description: China’s navy test-launched a long-range ballistic missile yesterday from one of its nuclear-powered submarines in the South Pacifi

item.title: Rabuka says Fiji expects no China pushback as Australia PM Albanese rejects rivalry claims
item.description: Fiji does not expect any pushback from China over the newly signed Vuvale Union Treaty with Australia, with Prime Minister Sitiveni Rabuka saying the agreement does not threaten either country's relationship with Beijing. Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has also rejected suggestions that the treaty is driven by Australia's efforts to counter China's growing influence in the Pacific, ins
item.content:encoded:
Fiji does not expect any pushback from China over the newly signed Vuvale Union Treaty with Australia, with Prime Minister Sitiveni Rabuka saying the agreement does not threaten either country's relationship with Beijing.

Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has also rejected suggestions that the treaty is driven by Australia's efforts to counter China's growing influence in the Pacific, insisting the agreement is about strengthening a longstanding partnership with Fiji.

Responding to the media in a joint press conference with Prime Minister Sitiveni Rabuka following the signing of the Vuvale Union Treaty and the Ocean of Peace Alliance, Albanese says the alliance builds on decades of defence cooperation and mutual trust between the two countries.

He says the agreement elevates an already close relationship to the highest level of strategic partnership.

Insert: Albanese - All about alliance July 07

He notes that Rabuka and several members of his Cabinet had undertaken military training in Australia and says the two countries had stood side by side on peacekeeping missions for many years.

He also clarified the treaty's mutual defence commitment, saying Article 6 provides that if Fiji is attacked by an external force, Australia will provide its full support.

Meanwhile, Rabuka also addressed concerns about Fiji's sovereignty under the new alliance, saying the country's independence remains protected under international law and through its regional partnerships.

Rabuka says Fiji's membership of the United Nations , together with its relationships in the Pacific, provides confidence that its sovereignty will be respected.

Insert: Rabuka - Ensure that our sovereignty is respected July 07

The Vuvale Union Treaty and Ocean of Peace Alliance is a major partnership agreement between Australia and Fiji focused on strengthening economic ties, security cooperation, and government collaboration.

The treaty is backed by more than $1.5 billion in investment over the next decade, including $1.05 billion in grants and $550 million in concessional loans .

It includes $284 million to strengthen policing and combat transnational crime and illicit drugs, $158 million to improve healthcare and respond to the HIV crisis, $778 million for infrastructure projects such as ports and flood protection, $146.3 million for digital transformation, and $57.5 million to strengthen economic resilience.

The agreement also expands opportunities for Fijians through improved visa access, $46 million to support student access to Australian universities from 2029, $35 million for sports development, including rugby union, and $15.5 million to strengthen cultural and faith-based ties.

item.title: FICAC ready for August trial in Riyaz Sayed-Khaiyum and Sagar legal fee case
item.description: FICAC lawyer Setefano Komaibaba has informed the court that the prosecution is ready to proceed to trial from 24th August in the legal fee case involving former Fijian Broadcasting Corporation Chief Executive Officer Riyaz Sayed-Khaiyum and current Chief Financial Officer Vimlesh Sagar. Komaibaba says the prosecution expects to call 10 to 15 witnesses during the trial.
item.content:encoded:
FICAC lawyer Setefano Komaibaba has informed the court that the prosecution is ready to proceed to trial from 24th August in the legal fee case involving former Fijian Broadcasting Corporation Chief Executive Officer Riyaz Sayed-Khaiyum and current Chief Financial Officer Vimlesh Sagar.

Komaibaba says the prosecution expects to call 10 to 15 witnesses during the trial.

Sayed-Khaiyum’s counsel, Gul Fatima, says they have not received a response to their correspondence dated 18th November last year, but with the new FICAC counsel, they hope to receive one.

Sagar’s counsel, Ritesh Singh, says they do not have any agreed facts.

Komaibaba says the defence can prepare another set of agreed facts, which he can review and respond to.

Sayed-Khaiyum is charged with abuse of office for gain and one count of general dishonesty causing a loss.

It is alleged that between 2017 and 2024, he pursued five legal cases without board approval, resulting in a loss of $138,813.37.

Sagar faces one count of general dishonesty causing a loss for allegedly approving payments totalling $15,075.88 to R Patel Lawyers without board approval in 2023.

The matter has been adjourned to 31st July.

item.title: Church College of Fiji marks 50 years with week-long Golden Jubilee celebrations
item.description: The school, formerly known as LDS Technical College, was founded in 1976 and is marking the milestone with a week-long programme under the theme “50 Years of Discipleship.” He says some former students had not seen their classmates for 5, 10, 20 or even 30 years, making the reunions a significant part of the celebrations.
item.content:encoded:
The school, formerly known as LDS Technical College, was founded in 1976 and is marking the milestone with a week-long programme under the theme “50 Years of Discipleship.”

He says some former students had not seen their classmates for 5, 10, 20 or even 30 years, making the reunions a significant part of the celebrations.

Today is a free day for participants, particularly the alumni who have travelled from overseas.

Tomorrow, the alumni will gather at 8.30am before marching from the Suva Flea Market to the Suva foreshore from around 9am.

Delaibatiki says a community service project will follow at the foreshore in collaboration with the Suva City Council, with activities expected to finish around 2pm.

A free public bazaar will then be held from 6pm to 11pm tomorrow, featuring alumni vendors selling different types of food, including barbecue, curries and other varieties, as well as clothing, alumni merchandise and handicrafts.

He says entertainment will also be provided by an alumna of LDS Technical College, a local artist, and her friends.

On Thursday morning, members of the church will attend a special temple session at the Fiji Temple in Samabula.

This will be followed later in the day by a cultural show at the school campus, featuring performances from alumni, their families and students from different regions in Fiji.

The cultural show will run from 6pm to 9pm, followed by a combined school dance from 9pm to 11pm, bringing together alumni and current students.

On Friday, a sports day organised by students will begin at 8.30am at the campus, with the alumni invited to join the students.

The school’s senior rugby team will then face RKS for the Te Aki Cup at 3pm at the Tamavua campus.

One of the biggest events of the week, the Golden Jubilee gala and ball, will be held on Friday evening, with alumni attending from the United States, New Zealand, Australia, other parts of the world and Fiji.

Delaibatiki says the gala and ball will feature entertainment from the Republic of Fiji Military Forces Band and other alumni of the school.

On Saturday, individual classes will hold their own picnics and activities before the week-long celebration concludes on Sunday with a devotional church service at 4pm at the school campus, featuring former administrators, students and alumni.

Reflecting on the school’s history, Delaibatiki says one major sporting milestone came in 1986 when the school won both the Under-18 and Under-19 Deans competitions.

He says the school also reached the finals around that period and had strong rugby and basketball teams, while several students went on to become athletes, musicians and professionals in vocational and trade industries.

Delaibatiki says the “50 Years of Discipleship” theme is centred on being a good person and making a positive contribution to society.

He says positive change begins with individuals working on themselves and making a positive impact on the people around them, which can then create a ripple effect across communities.

Delaibatiki says Fiji needs more of that positive energy, particularly as young people face different social issues.

He also urged people to make the most of every opportunity to spend time with family and friends, saying life is short and no one knows when a meeting with someone close to them could be the last.

item.title: Live tsunami siren tests underway along Suva-Lami corridor
item.description: A live tsunami siren test is currently underway along the Suva-Lami corridor as the National Disaster Risk Management Office tests

item.title: iTLTB seeks return of Freehold Land not rightfully obtained, mineral and fisheries rights to rightful landowners
item.description: The iTaukei Land Trust Board is calling for all freehold land that was not rightfully obtained to be returned to its original land

item.title: Fiji secures $1.5b in Australian investment as Vuvale Treaty Union signed
item.description: Fiji has secured more than $1.5 billion in new Australian investment since the renewal of the Vuvale Partnership in October 2023,
